steps.title: Identify the main focus
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea, so the right choice must summarize the whole passage rather than one small detail. The passage first defines shimmering as a group defense behavior in giant honeybees. It then explains when the behavior occurs and why researchers think it matters.

steps.title: Connect behavior to purpose
steps.content: The key relationship is between hornets and shimmering. Hornets hovering near a colony initiate the wave-like body movements, and the behavior deters the hornets from approaching. The passage also says shimmering is not observed when other, larger predators approach, which supports the researchers' view that the behavior is specialized for hornets.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A shifts to uncertainty about defenses against larger predators, but the passage only says shimmering is not used then. Choice C calls hornets the main predator, which is never stated. Choice D adds other insect species, though only giant honeybees are discussed. The choice about shimmering as a specific defense against hornets best captures the whole passage. The answer is B.
